name: Ganciclovir
executiveSummary: Antiviral for cytomegalovirus in transplant recipients and immunocompromised patients; myelotoxicity.
indications:
## Indications

- Invasive CMV disease in transplant recipients.
- CMV prophylaxis in high-risk regimens.

## Precautions

- Neutropenia and thrombocytopenia. Teratogenic. Adjust in chronic kidney disease.

> Adjust per institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Lyophilized powder vial containing 500 mg (Ganciclovir Richet, Cymevene).
adulto.diluent: 500 mg in 50–100 mL of 0.9% NaCl or 5% Dextrose.
adulto.administration: Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute in 100 mL of 0.9% NaCl or 5% Dextrose and administer in no less than 60 min.

pediatrico.dose: 5 mg/kg IV every 12 h per transplant protocol.

neonatal.dose: 6 mg/kg/dose every 12 h in congenital CMV (specialized protocol).

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 12 h at room temperature. Do not refrigerate.

## Diluted solution (to be administered)

- 24 h at room temperature. Do not refrigerate.

bibliography.citation: Infectious Diseases, Infection Prevention and Control Service. UCIP 2026 — Dilution and stability guide.
Infectious Diseases Society of America (IDSA). Clinical guidelines.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
Society of Critical Care Medicine (SCCM). ICU medication guidelines.
